records.ety: [OF. court, curt, cort, F. cour, LL. cortis, fr. L. cohors, cors, chors, gen. cohortis, cortis, chortis, an inclosure, court, thing inclosed, crowd, throng; co- + a root akin to Gr. chorto`s inclosure, feeding place, and to E. garden, yard, orchard. See Yard, and cf. Cohort, Curtain.]

defs: An inclosed space; a courtyard; an uncovered area shut in by the walls of a building, or by different building; also, a space opening from a street and nearly surrounded by houses; a blind alley.
quotes: The courts of the house of our God.
And round the cool green courts there ran a row Of cloisters.
Goldsmith took a garret in a miserable court.

defs: The residence of a sovereign, prince, nobleman, or other dignitary; a palace.
quotes: Attends the emperor in his royal court.
This our court, infected with their manners, Shows like a riotous inn.

defs: The collective body of persons composing the retinue of a sovereign or person high in authority; all the surroundings of a sovereign in his regal state.
quotes: My lord, there is a nobleman of the court at door would speak with you.
Love rules the court, the camp, the grove.

defs: Any formal assembling of the retinue of a sovereign; as, to hold a court.
quotes: The princesses held their court within the fortress.

defs: Attention directed to a person in power; conduct or address designed to gain favor; courtliness of manners; civility; compliment; flattery.
quotes: No solace could her paramour intreat Her once to show, ne court, nor dalliance.
I went to make my court to the Duke and Duchess of Newcastle.

defs: The hall, chamber, or place, where justice is administered.

The persons officially assembled under authority of law, at the appropriate time and place, for the administration of justice; an official assembly, legally met together for the transaction of judicial business; a judge or judges sitting for the hearing or trial of causes.
A tribunal established for the administration of justice.
The judge or judges; as distinguished from the counsel or jury, or both.
quotes: Most heartily I do beseech the court To give the judgment.

defs: The session of a judicial assembly.
Any jurisdiction, civil, military, or ecclesiastical.
A place arranged for playing the game of tennis; also, one of the divisions of a tennis court.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Courted; p. pr. & vb. n. Courting.]

defs: To endeavor to gain the favor of by attention or flattery; to try to ingratiate one's self with.
quotes: By one person, hovever, Portland was still assiduously courted.

defs: To endeavor to gain the affections of; to seek in marriage; to woo.
quotes: If either of you both love Katharina . . . Leave shall you have to court her at your pleasure.

defs: To attempt to gain; to solicit; to seek.
quotes: They might almost seem to have courted the crown of martyrdom.
Guilt and misery . . . court privacy and solitude.

defs: To invite by attractions; to allure; to attract.
quotes: A well-worn pathway courted us To one green wicket in a privet hedge.

defs: To play the lover; to woo; as, to go courting.
